subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Routes of Exposure
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Routes of Exposure.

hook.text: You can be poisoned through your mouth, skin, lungs, or even your eyes. Four doors in.

buildup.text: Ingestion, inhalation, absorption, and injection are the four routes of toxic exposure.

discovery.text: Inhalation is the fastest route—gases reach the bloodstream through the lungs in seconds.

twist.text: Some chemicals penetrate intact skin—organophosphate pesticides can poison through touch alone.

climax.text: Knowing the exposure route determines the correct first aid response.

punchline.text: How the poison got in determines how you get it out.

quiz.question: Which route of toxic exposure reaches the bloodstream fastest?
